title: Parsons Square End Table
summary: A simple square Parsons-style end table or nightstand built from two shelves and a 2x2 frame — the kind of build worth taking slowly for the finish, not the joinery.
description:
The joinery here is genuinely simple — two pocket-holed shelves joined to a 2x2 frame — but the source flags something worth taking seriously: bring your wood into the room it will live in and let it acclimate overnight before cutting. A humidity swing between garage and living room can move solid lumber enough to open a gap in a simple butt joint like this one, and there's no joinery trick that fixes wood that's still moving.

The top and bottom shelves can be built from a single 1x10, or from two 3/4" plywood panels cut to 18-1/2" square — plywood won't move with humidity the way solid 1x10 can, which is worth considering if the table will sit somewhere the humidity isn't stable.

materials.name: Pine, 1x10, 8 ft
materials.note: Two 3/4" plywood or project panels, 18-1/2" x 18-1/2" each, work as a substitute.

steps.title: Acclimate the wood
steps.body: Bring your wood into the room the table will live in and let it acclimate overnight before cutting or building — this project needs a humidity-controlled interior space with no big swings, or joints can open a gap as the wood adjusts.

steps.title: Build the two shelves
steps.body: Build two shelves by joining boards together with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, three screws per joint. Keep the outside edges flush.

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Drill three 3/4" pocket holes per side around each shelf, for attaching to the frame later. Measure and cut the frame pieces and attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws so the top edges are flush. Drill a 1-1/2" pocket hole in one hole on each end, on the bottom, of the frame pieces.

steps.title: Join the side frames
steps.body: Attach the side frames together with 1-1/2" pocket holes, 2-1/2" pocket hole screws, and glue — one screw per joint, hiding pocket holes on the undersides.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body:
Attach the two shelves to the side frames with pocket hole screws and glue, keeping all outside edges flush.

Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Parsons square end table with two open shelves
